scenario_results.reason: Architecture guidance stays at the warehouse layer and explains workload isolation, layout, and cost-performance tradeoffs explicitly.
Modeling recommendation compares star-schema and denormalized marts using warehouse-specific usability, maintenance, and query tradeoffs.
Replay plan addresses late-arriving facts, idempotent reload, referential checks, and post-replay validation.
Schema-drift response defines contract checks, compatibility gates, and safe rollout or stop criteria.
Reconciliation guidance maps counts, sums, duplicates, thresholds, and owner actions to warehouse trust decisions.
Cost-performance advice is evidence-backed and tied to warehouse layout, workload isolation, and materialization choices rather than generic tuning.
Restore runbook includes rollback triggers, restore mechanics, and integrity verification within stated recovery objectives.
Governance-sensitive handling stays technical, states assumptions, and escalates policy interpretation instead of overreaching.
Recommendation quality is tied to cited vendor and public-standard sources with explicit uncertainty and tradeoffs.
Adjacent reporting work is rejected clearly with a narrow routing rationale back to the orchestrator.
Low-confidence requests are handled by asking for missing evidence and refusing to guess.
AI-use scenario escalates model-governance ownership while preserving warehouse data stewardship responsibilities.

notes: Scenario count meets or exceeds minimum_scenario_count (12 >= 12).
Accuracy acceptance met, no unreviewed regressions recorded, and human verification is reflected in readiness evidence.
Coverage includes normal design guidance, governance handling, pipeline and model-adjacent failure modes, evidence-backed recommendations, out-of-scope rejection, and low-confidence escalation.
